title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of 03-31-2026
content:
NORTHERN VALLEY's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances declined -1.7% from the previous period. Total loans of $12,167 thousand remain concentrated in Used Vehicle and New Vehicle, which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk.

Portfolio Composition
NORTHERN VALLEY's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in Used Vehicle. As of 03-31-2026, Used Vehicle totaled $5,914 thousand (48.6%) and New Vehicle totaled $2,263 thousand (18.6%), together representing approximately 67.2% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition
All Other Secured Non-Real Estate accounted for $3,093 thousand (25.4%). Overall loan balances declined -1.7% from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans fell to an amount not explicitly quantified in thousands on this page but represented a decrease of 4.2% from the previous quarter. The largest delinquency exposure remains in Used Vehicle and New Vehicle.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
The concentration of delinquencies within vehicle-related segments remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
